title: US-Iran Talks Face New Challenges as Tensions Rise in Switzerland
subtitle: Negotiations in Switzerland continue despite walkouts and threats over the Strait of Hormuz.

summary.en: High-stakes negotiations between the United States and Iran have hit a difficult period in Switzerland. Iranian officials walked out of the talks to protest aggressive social media posts from President Donald Trump. The dispute centers on the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping lane that Iran claims to have closed. Meanwhile, fighting continues in southern Lebanon between Israeli forces and Hezbollah. Mediators from Qatar and Pakistan are working to keep the diplomatic process alive. Both sides remain under pressure to honor a recent agreement to end the war.
summary.zh: 美國與伊朗之間的高風險談判在瑞士陷入了艱難時期。伊朗官員為抗議川普總統在社群媒體上的激進貼文而憤而離席。爭議的核心在於荷莫茲海峽，這是一條伊朗聲稱已關閉的重要航運水道。與此同時，以色列軍隊與黎巴嫩真主黨在黎巴嫩南部的戰鬥仍在持續。來自卡達與巴基斯坦的調停者正努力維持外交進程。雙方仍面臨必須履行近期停戰協議的壓力。

paragraphs.en: High-stakes negotiations between the United States and Iran have reached a difficult stage in Switzerland. While both sides arrived in the country with the goal of saving a fragile 60-day peace agreement, the talks were interrupted when the Iranian delegation walked out in protest. According to The Guardian, the Iranian team left the meeting following a series of aggressive threats made by US President Donald Trump on social media. Despite this, mediators from Qatar and Pakistan continue to work in the background to keep the diplomatic process moving.
paragraphs.zh: 美國與伊朗之間的高風險談判在瑞士陷入了艱難階段。儘管雙方抵達該國時皆以挽救一項脆弱的六十天和平協議為目標，但隨著伊朗代表團憤而離席抗議，談判被迫中斷。根據《衛報》報導，伊朗團隊是在美國總統川普於社群媒體上發出一連串激進威脅後離開會議的。儘管如此，來自卡達與巴基斯坦的調停者仍在幕後持續努力，以維持外交進程的運作。

paragraphs.en: The current crisis is linked to the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow and vital shipping channel. Approximately 20% of the world’s oil and natural gas passes through this area, making it essential for the global economy. Last week, both nations signed a memorandum of understanding—a formal agreement that outlines the goals of two parties—to lift a blockade on the strait. However, Iran announced it had closed the waterway again, claiming this was a response to ongoing Israeli military strikes in southern Lebanon. BBC News reports that while Iran claims the strait is shut, tracking data shows that ships are still moving through the area.
paragraphs.zh: 目前的危機與荷莫茲海峽有關，這是一條狹窄且至關重要的航運水道。全球約百分之二十的石油與天然氣經由此處運輸，使其對全球經濟至關重要。上週，兩國簽署了一份諒解備忘錄——即一份概述雙方目標的正式協議——旨在解除對該海峽的封鎖。然而，伊朗宣稱為了回應以色列在黎巴嫩南部的持續軍事打擊，已再次關閉該水道。英國廣播公司新聞網報導指出，儘管伊朗聲稱海峽已關閉，但追蹤數據顯示船隻仍持續在該區域通行。

paragraphs.en: The situation has been made worse by a public war of words. President Trump recently warned that he would hit Iran hard if it did not stop its "proxies" in Lebanon. In this context, proxies are armed groups that receive support from a country to fight on its behalf. Trump also suggested that the US might take control of the strait if a deal is not reached. According to The Guardian, these comments caused significant anger among the Iranian team, who felt their personal safety was at risk. Iranian chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf dismissed the threats, stating that the Americans would not be in their current position if such language actually worked.
paragraphs.zh: 公開的口水戰使局勢更加惡化。川普總統近期警告，若伊朗不停止其在黎巴嫩的「代理人」行動，他將對伊朗採取強硬手段。在此脈絡下，代理人是指接受國家支持並代其作戰的武裝團體。川普亦暗示，若無法達成協議，美國可能會接管該海峽。根據《衛報》報導，這些言論引起伊朗團隊的強烈憤怒，他們認為自身的人身安全受到威脅。伊朗首席談判代表穆罕默德·巴吉爾·卡利巴夫駁斥了這些威脅，並表示如果這種言語真的有效，美國人就不會處於目前的困境。

paragraphs.en: In contrast to the president’s aggressive tone, US Vice-President JD Vance has tried to keep the focus on finding a new path forward. Before the walkout, Vance noted that he had been asked by President Trump to "turn over a new leaf" in the relationship between the two countries. He suggested that if Iran stops being a source of regional instability and gives up its nuclear ambitions, the US is willing to change its relationship with the country. However, these diplomatic efforts are complicated by the reality on the ground. Fighting continues in southern Lebanon, where Israeli forces remain despite a ceasefire—an agreement to stop fighting—that was meant to end the violence.
paragraphs.zh: 與總統的激進語氣形成對比的是，美國副總統范斯試圖將焦點放在尋找新的前進道路上。在代表團離席前，范斯提到他已受川普總統之託，要在兩國關係上「翻開新的一頁」。他表示，如果伊朗不再是區域不穩定的根源並放棄核武野心，美國願意改變與該國的關係。然而，這些外交努力受到地面現實情況的複雜化影響。黎巴嫩南部的戰鬥仍在持續，儘管雙方曾達成旨在結束暴力的停火協議，以色列軍隊仍駐紮於該地。

paragraphs.en: There is also significant domestic pressure on both sides. The Iranian delegation is facing criticism at home, with many citizens and officials distrusting the American negotiating team. Meanwhile, the US president is dealing with political pressure from both major political parties. The complexity of the situation was further highlighted by a recent explosion at a factory in the Ras Laffan industrial zone in Qatar. While Qatari authorities described it as a "technical incident," the site had been damaged in previous fighting, reminding everyone how easily the regional situation can change.
paragraphs.zh: 雙方也都面臨巨大的國內壓力。伊朗代表團在國內面臨批評，許多公民與官員對美國談判團隊缺乏信任。與此同時，美國總統也正處理來自兩大主要政黨的政治壓力。近期卡達拉斯拉凡工業區一座工廠發生的爆炸事件，進一步凸顯了局勢的複雜性。雖然卡達當局將其描述為「技術性事故」，但該地點在先前的戰鬥中曾受損，這提醒了所有人區域局勢是多麼容易發生變化。

paragraphs.en: Despite the walkout, there are signs that some progress is still possible. According to The Guardian, the two sides managed to reach a draft agreement on how the US will issue a waiver. A waiver is an official document that allows a country to ignore a rule, in this case, the sanctions—official penalties used to force a country to follow international law—that currently block Iranian oil exports. Iranian officials believe these waivers will be issued soon and that progress has been made on unfreezing Iranian money held in overseas bank accounts.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管發生了離席事件，仍有跡象顯示取得進展是有可能的。根據《衛報》報導，雙方已針對美國如何發布豁免權達成協議草案。豁免權是一份官方文件，允許國家無視某項規定，在此案例中，即指目前阻礙伊朗石油出口的制裁——即用以強迫國家遵守國際法的官方懲罰措施。伊朗官員認為這些豁免權將很快發布，且在解凍伊朗存放於海外銀行帳戶的資金方面也已取得進展。

paragraphs.en: As the talks continue, the international community is watching closely. The Qatari prime minister has stated that he welcomes the continuation of the talks, emphasizing the importance of finding a peaceful solution. For now, the future of the 60-day peace agreement remains uncertain. Both the US and Iran must decide if they can move past their public arguments to address the deeper issues causing the conflict. With the global economy depending on the stability of the Strait of Hormuz, the stakes for both nations and the rest of the world remain very high.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著談判持續，國際社會正密切關注。卡達總理表示歡迎談判繼續進行，並強調尋求和平解決方案的重要性。目前，這項六十天和平協議的未來仍充滿不確定性。美國與伊朗必須決定，他們是否能超越公開的爭執，去解決導致衝突的深層問題。由於全球經濟仰賴荷莫茲海峽的穩定，這對兩國乃至全世界而言，賭注依然非常巨大。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To leave a meeting or event as a sign of protest or disagreement.
keyTerms.definitionZh: （因抗議或不滿而）中途退席、離場。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於描述走路，這裡指抗議離場。文中：the talks were interrupted when the Iranian delegation walked out in protest.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A situation where two sides exchange angry or critical statements publicly.
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面上是戰爭，實指公開的言語對抗。文中：The situation has been made worse by a public war of words.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To start behaving in a better or more positive way.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 改過自新、重新做人、翻開新的一頁。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思與實際含義無關，指改變態度或關係。文中：Vance noted that he had been asked by President Trump to "turn over a new leaf" in the relationship between the two countries.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The risks involved in a situation; what can be gained or lost.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（木樁），這裡指事件的重要性與風險。文中：With the global economy depending on the stability of the Strait of Hormuz, the stakes for both nations and the rest of the world remain very high.

sources.name: BBC News
sources.title: Trump and Iran's negotiator trade warnings as talks held in Switzerland

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: US-Iran talks strained as Trump threats spark Iranian walkout

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: US, Iran hold talks in Switzerland while Strait of Hormuz remains shut

previousArticles.title: US and Iran Hold High-Stakes Talks in Switzerland Amid Regional Tensions
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United States and Iran have begun direct talks in Switzerland to save a fragile 60-day peace agreement. Tensions remain high as President Trump threatened further action against Iran over its support for Hezbollah. Iran recently claimed to have closed the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping route, in response to ongoing Israeli military strikes. Despite these threats, international tracking data shows that ships are still moving through the waterway. Both nations are now working with mediators to address regional instability and nuclear concerns.

previousArticles.title: Tensions Rise as Iran Threatens to Close Vital Shipping Route
previousArticles.summaryEn: Iran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response to recent Israeli military strikes in Lebanon. This move threatens a fragile 60-day ceasefire agreement signed between the US and Iran just days ago. While Iran claims the waterway is closed, the US military insists that traffic is still moving normally. International leaders are now heading to Switzerland to continue peace talks despite the rising violence. The situation remains uncertain as both sides struggle to maintain the new peace deal.

quiz.question: What was the primary reason for the Iranian delegation's decision to walk out of the negotiations in Switzerland?

options.A: They were dissatisfied with the terms of the 60-day peace agreement.
options.B: They felt threatened by aggressive comments made by the US President on social media.
options.C: They were protesting the ongoing military strikes in southern Lebanon.
options.D: They were responding to the explosion at a factory in the Ras Laffan industrial zone.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第一段與第三段，伊朗代表團是因為美國總統在社交媒體上發布了具侵略性的威脅言論，導致他們感到人身安全受到威脅才憤而離場。選項 A 錯誤，因為他們原本是為了挽救該協議而去的；選項 C 雖然提到黎巴嫩衝突，但那是伊朗宣稱關閉海峽的理由，而非離場的原因；選項 D 的工廠爆炸發生在卡達，與代表團離場無直接因果關係。

quiz.question: Based on the article, what can be inferred about the current status of the Strait of Hormuz?

options.A: The strait is completely impassable due to a successful Iranian blockade.
options.B: The memorandum of understanding has effectively ended all tensions in the region.
options.C: The actual physical movement of ships contradicts official Iranian claims about the waterway.
options.D: The US has already taken control of the strait to ensure global oil supplies.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第二段，雖然伊朗宣布關閉海峽，但 BBC 的追蹤數據顯示船隻仍在通行，這顯示伊朗的官方說法與實際情況不符。選項 A 錯誤，因為數據顯示船隻仍可通行；選項 B 錯誤，因為文章顯示局勢依然緊張且充滿變數；選項 D 錯誤，文章僅提到美國總統「威脅」可能會接管，並未實際執行。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the phrase 'turn over a new leaf' mean in the context of US-Iran relations?

options.A: To start acting in a fresh, more positive way.
options.B: To hide secret documents from the other party.
options.C: To physically remove all military forces from the region.
options.D: To repeat the same negotiation mistakes from the past.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。片語 'turn over a new leaf' 的意思是「改過自新」或「翻開新的一頁」，在此語境下指副總統希望美伊關係能有新的、正向的開始。選項 B 與原文無關；選項 C 是具體的軍事行動，非該片語的隱喻含義；選項 D 的意思與該片語的正面含義完全相反。

quiz.question: What is the central message of this article regarding the current state of US-Iran negotiations?

options.A: The negotiations have completely failed, and war is now inevitable.
options.B: The US and Iran have successfully resolved their differences through the help of Qatar.
options.C: The negotiations are fragile and complex, caught between diplomatic progress and ongoing regional instability.
options.D: The main obstacle to peace is the lack of communication between the two nations' presidents.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文旨在描述美伊談判的複雜性，既有代表團離場、言詞交鋒等阻礙，也有達成豁免權草案等進展，且受到黎巴嫩衝突與國內政治壓力影響。選項 A 過於悲觀，文章仍提到有進展；選項 B 錯誤，雙方尚未解決歧見；選項 D 將問題簡化為溝通問題，忽略了深層的政治與區域衝突因素。
